name: Lucy's Salmon Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a large pot over medium heat.
2. Add the chopped onions and celery to the pot. Cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
3. Add the diced potatoes and enough water or broth to cover them by about an inch. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer until the potatoes are tender when pierced with a fork, approximately 20-25 minutes.
4. Remove from heat and set aside.
5. Melt the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter in a saucepan over medium heat.
6. Whisk in the flour to form a roux. Cook for about 1 minute, stirring constantly, until the roux is light golden brown.
7. Gradually whisk in the milk, stirring constantly to avoid lumps, until well combined and smooth.
8. Stir in the evaporated tomatoes, thyme, dillweed, white pepper, and parsley. Simmer gently for about 5 minutes.
9. Add the canned salmon, reserving any juices. Gently stir the soup to combine.
10. If desired, stir in the grated Monterey Jack cheese until melted and incorporated into the soup.
11. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Serve hot, garnished with additional parsley or a sprinkle of paprika, if desired.
This recipe is designed to be clear, concise, and easy to follow while maintaining all original ingredients and steps.
